title: -Contract: West Coast Drilling, Inc. for 2709 Hilgard Avenue Retaining Wall Stabilization

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during recess to
execute a contract with West Coast Drilling, Inc. for design-build services for urgent
slope and retaining wall stabilization work at 2709 Hilgard Avenue, in the amount of
$585,000 which includes a contingency of $88,631.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
Staff have determined that there is an imminent risk of damage to public and private
property due to ongoing retaining wall failure and slope instability at 2709 Hilgard
Avenue and that this risk warrants immediate action. Delaying contract award until after
Council recess exacerbates this risk to health and property due to potential for
increased instability during winter rains.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the Project is available in FY 2026 from the State Transportation Tax (Fund
127), Clean Storm Water Fund (616), and the Sanitary Sewer Operations Fund (611).
No other funding is required, and no other projects will be delayed due to this
expenditure.
Design-build estimate ...............................................................................$496,369
Contingency................................................................................................$88,631

sections.current_situation:
The City Manager, under advice of the Public Works Department and City Attorney’s
Office staff, deemed the work to repair the retaining wall and stabilize the hillside in front
of 2709 Hilgard an emergency and of urgent necessity for the preservation of life, health
and property. Per City Charter § 67.4(a), this situation meets the conditions for waiving
competitive solicitation requirements.
Public Works obtained an estimate from West Coast Drilling, Inc. for the design and
construction of improvements to stabilize the slope and retaining wall for an amount of
$496,639. Staff recommend awarding a non-competitive design-build services contract
to West Coast Drilling, Inc. based on its performance as a subcontractor on
competitively bid projects and its experience with slope stabilization work, including
performing emergency slope stabilization and retaining wall work for the City along
Wildcat Canyon Road.
The scope of the authorized work is anticipated to include:
 Structural engineering;
 Drilled support piers and a structural grade beam to stabilize the hillside and
existing retaining structure;
 Shotcrete facing to reinforce and stabilize the wall;
 Retaining wall drainage and surface drainage improvements; and
 Backfilling, compaction, and resurfacing of the area supported by the wall.
sections.background:
A retaining wall of unknown origin, located on unimproved right-of-way in front of 2709
Hilgard Avenue failed. Retaining wall fill material has migrated through cracks in the
wall, forming a sinkhole that has made a section of Hilgard Road impassable to drivers
and others and has caused slope instability that threatens the residence at 2709 Hilgard
Road. With the onset of the wet season, increasing soil saturation and elevated
groundwater levels may accelerate the migration of backfill through existing cracks,
further undermining the roadway supported by the wall. Continued settlement presents
a growing risk of structural damage, with a resulting risk of liability to the City. Instability
of the wall also threatens the pedestrian pathway and adjacent downslope properties.
These risks are exacerbated by the site’s proximity to a trace of the Hayward Fault,
adding a seismic vulnerability component to the existing slope instability.
In accordance with the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) requirements, this
project has been determined to be categorically exempt because it pertains to
emergency repairs.
The Living Wage Ordinance does not apply to this project since Public Works
construction contracts are, pursuant to City policy, subject to State prevailing wage
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
West Coast Drilling for 2709 Hilgard Avenue Retaining Wall Stabilization CONSENT CALENDAR
January 27, 2026
laws. The Community Workforce Agreement (CWA) does not apply to this contract
because the estimated project value does not exceed $500,000.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The slope stabilization that this project provides would reduce sediment entering the
storm drain downstream. Though no creek is downstream of the site, this would provide
a water quality benefit. Additionally, safe and usable pathways and roads encourage
community members to more regularly walk and cycle, reducing greenhouse gas
emissions from cars.
sections.rationale:
Awarding the design-build contract to West Cost Drilling, Inc. and expediting emergency
repairs abate hazardous conditions to life, health, and property. Performing the work
requires contracted services, as the City does not possess the in-house labor,
equipment, or expertise necessary to complete the project.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative actions were considered for this project. Further deterioration of the
retaining wall and expansion of the sinkhole above would place public and private
infrastructure at risk.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Department of Public Works, (510) 981-6396
Ricardo Salcedo, Acting Supervising Civil Engineer, Public Works, (510) 981-6407

1: Resolution
Page 3

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: WEST COAST DRILLING, INC. FOR 2709 HILGARD AVENUE
RETAINING WALL STABILIZATION
WHEREAS, a retaining wall of unknown origin, located on unimproved right-of-way in
front of 2709 Hilgard Avenue has failed, forming a sinkhole that has made a section of
Hilgard Road impassable to drivers and others and has caused slope instability that
threatens the residence at 2709 Hilgard Road as well as the pedestrian pathway and
residences below; and
WHEREAS, the City Manager deemed the work to repair the retaining wall and stabilize
the hillside an emergency and of urgent necessity for the preservation of life, health and
property and per City Charter § 67.4(a), this situation meets the conditions for waiving
competitive solicitation requirements; and
WHEREAS, the City received an estimate from West Coast Drilling, Inc. for the design
and construction of improvements to stabilize the slope and retaining wall for an amount
not to exceed $496,639; and
WHEREAS, staff recommend awarding a design-build services contract to West Coast
Drilling, Inc. based on its performance as a subcontractor on competitively bid projects
and its experience with slope stabilization work, including performing emergency slope
stabilization and retaining wall work for the City along Wildcat Canyon Road.; and
WHEREAS, funding for the emergency project is available in FY 2026 from the State
Transportation Tax (Fund 127), Clean Storm Water Fund (616), and the Sanitary Sewer
Operations Fund (611). No other funding is required, and no other projects will be
delayed due to this expenditure.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager to execute a contract with West
Coast Drilling, Inc. for the design and construction of retaining wall stabilization
improvements in an amount not to exceed $585,000, which includes an $88,361
contingency for unforeseen circumst
